message:
perf(count): use lean stats rows for grouped counts when safe

`br count --by ...` was hydrating the full Issue rows via `list_issues` and
then walking them in memory just to bucket by status/priority/type/assignee/
label. For workspaces with thousands of issues this duplicated the per-row
allocation cost paid by `list` even though we only need a handful of fields.

This commit reuses the lean `StatsIssueRow` path that the recent count perf
work introduced for the simple total case, but generalizes it to the grouped
case as well:

- `group_counts_for_filters` is the new dispatcher. It checks
  `should_use_full_issue_rows_for_count` — currently true only for
  `title_contains` (FTS) and the explicit empty-string `assignee=""` filter,
  which the stats query cannot evaluate — and otherwise falls through to the
  stats path.
- `stats_row_matches_count_filters` reproduces the subset of `ListFilters`
  semantics that StatsIssueRow can answer locally: status/type/priority
  allowlists, assignee equality, the unassigned flag, the
  `include_closed` / `include_deferred` / `include_templates` rules, and the
  tombstone-hide-by-default behavior. The intent is to match
  `list_issues_filtered` exactly for everything we route through this path.
- `group_stats_counts` is the lean version of `group_counts`: status/priority/
  type/assignee buckets read directly off the stats row, and the label path
  bulk-fetches labels for just the surviving IDs via
  `storage.get_labels_for_issues`, preserving the "(no labels)" sentinel.

Tests:
- `test_lean_group_counts_match_full_issue_filtering` plants a mix of open,
  closed (with `closed_at`), deferred, and template issues, runs the same
  filters through both the legacy `list_issues + group_counts` path and the
  new `group_counts_for_filters`, and asserts the totals + per-group counts
  agree.
- `test_lean_group_counts_include_labels_for_filtered_rows` covers the
  assignee-filter + label-grouping crossover, which was the most likely place
  for the lean path to silently disagree with the legacy path.
- Extracted `groups_to_map` helper and reused it in the existing status/label
  tests to drop the duplicated `BTreeMap::insert` loops.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
